description: Custom field API names must be composed of one or more Title Case tokens optionally separated by underscores and must end with __c. Single-token names without underscores are valid.
Field API names must not start with technical prefixes such as txt_, str_, num_, int_, dec_, dt_, dtm_, cb_, bln_, pl_, fx_.
Field metadata must contain a non-empty <description> element.

rule: Field inline help text is mandatory
description: Field metadata must contain a non-empty <inlineHelpText> element.
If a token contains internal CamelCase (e.g., IsValidText__c), it may represent multiple words not separated by underscores. Word boundaries cannot be proven without a dictionary.
If field type is Checkbox, the API name must start with Is_ or Has_.
If field type is Date, the API name must end with _Date__c.
If field type is DateTime, the API name must end with _At__c.
If field type is Time, the API name must end with _Time__c.
If field type is Currency, API name is expected to end with _Amount__c according to semantic convention.
If field type is Percent, API name is expected to end with _Pct__c according to semantic convention.
Numeric fields that represent counts should end with _Count__c. Intent cannot be inferred without tagging or dictionary.
If a field is marked as External ID (<externalId>true</externalId>), the API name must end with _Ext_Id__c.
External ID fields are recommended to be unique, but this depends on integration design.
Lookup fields should be named by business role rather than generic object name. This cannot be validated without business context.

Picklist fields must have their value source resolvable as one of: inline values (<valueSetDefinition>), referenced value set (<valueSetName>), or StandardValueSet resolved from the StandardValueSets folder when the field metadata only declares type Picklist.
example: Inline: <valueSetDefinition>... | Referenced: <valueSetName>My_GVS</valueSetName> | Standard: resolve via StandardValueSets/

rule: Inline picklist labels in Title Case
description: For inline picklists only, each value <label> must be Title Case (each space- or hyphen-separated word starts with uppercase).

rule: Prefer Global Value Sets for reuse
description: If identical inline picklist value sets are detected across multiple fields, a Global Value Set may be more appropriate.
example: Warn when the same label set appears in multiple inline picklists

description: If field metadata contains a <formula> element, treat it as a Formula field regardless of its declared <type>.
Formula fields should explain derivation logic in the description, but enforcement depends on governance policy.
example: Prefer description like: "Derived from X and Y to compute Z"

description: Custom object metadata must contain a non-empty <description> element.
